name: The Riley State Bank Of Riley, Kansas
description_short: The Riley State Bank of Riley is a community bank serving Kansas with locations in Riley, Leonardville, Wakefield, and Longford, offering personal and business banking services.
description_long:
The Riley State Bank of Riley operates as a traditional community bank with a presence across multiple Kansas locations. The bank operates a 24/7 branch and also manages affiliated operations including Farmers & Merchants State Bank of Wakefield & Longford, KS, indicating regional consolidation and shared banking infrastructure across central Kansas communities. The bank has maintained operations long enough to establish itself as a local financial institution serving both personal and business customers in rural Kansas markets.

The bank offers a range of standard banking services including personal checking and savings accounts, business checking accounts, investment accounts, and ATM/debit card access through the SHAZAM network. For business customers, they provide tiered checking account options with different fee structures based on transaction volume and average balance maintenance. Personal customers have access to online banking tools, calculators for financial planning, and safe deposit box services available across all locations. The bank maintains traditional banking hours alongside 24/7 ATM and digital access.

The Riley State Bank distinguishes itself through community-focused banking with multiple physical locations in small Kansas towns, suggesting personalized service relationships typical of regional banks. The bank operates both under its Riley State Bank name and manages the separate Farmers & Merchants State Bank brand, indicating a consolidated operation serving multiple communities. Their emphasis on maintaining physical branches and safe deposit boxes reflects commitment to traditional banking services rather than digital-only operations.

As a community bank, the Riley State Bank offers stability and local decision-making but comes with higher fees compared to larger national banks or online banking alternatives. Business checking accounts carry monthly service charges ($5-$6) and per-check fees, which can be costly for high-transaction businesses. The bank's service offerings are fairly standard without specialized products like personal loans, credit cards, or wealth management services visible on their public website. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Business checking accounts with multiple tier options
SHAZAM ATM/Debit card with no usage fees
Safe deposit boxes at all branch locations
Monthly statements with check images mailed to customers
pros: 24/7 branch access for customer convenience and emergency banking needs
Multiple physical locations across Kansas (Riley, Leonardville, Wakefield, Longford) for local community service
No ATM fees for cash withdrawals, transfers, or balance inquiries at their own ATMs
No debit card usage fees when using SHAZAM network
Business checking accounts waive monthly service charge with $5,000+ average balance, providing cost incentive
Safe deposit boxes offered at all locations for document and valuables storage
Tiered business checking options (standard vs. RSB) allowing customers to choose fee structures based on usage
cons: Business checking monthly service charges ($5-$6) are higher than online bank alternatives
Per-check fees ($0.25) add up for businesses with high transaction volumes
Limited product range visible on website—no personal loans, credit cards, or investment advisory services advertised
Small community bank may lack advanced digital banking features or investment products of larger institutions
ATM card replacement and PIN change fees ($5 each) apply despite no ATM usage fees
best_for: Small business owners in central Kansas seeking local relationship banking with community decision-makers
Residents of Riley, Leonardville, Wakefield, or Longford needing convenient local branch access
Customers prioritizing physical banking locations and in-person service over digital-only platforms
Businesses with average balances above $5,000 that can waive monthly checking account service charges
diagnosis: The Riley State Bank of Riley is best suited for small business owners and residents in central Kansas communities who value local relationship banking and physical branch access. The main caveat is that business customers with high transaction volumes will face cumulative per-check fees, and the bank lacks the broader product range (personal loans, credit cards, wealth management) found at larger regional or national banks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Community bank serving Kansas with locations in Riley, Leonardville, Wakefield, and Longford, offering personal and business banking.
fdic_official_name: The Riley State Bank of Riley, Kansas
